Description: Fixed header stays visible on scroll — reduces navigation friction
Do: Use sticky header with backdrop blur for depth
Don't: Make header too tall (>64px) — steals content space
Code_Bad: <header style='position:fixed; height:120px'> — takes too much screen
A11y_Note: Landmark: <nav role='navigation' aria-label='Main'>
Performance_Note: backdrop-blur may cause repaint on scroll — test on low-end

Description: Header starts transparent over hero then becomes solid on scroll
Do: Transition opacity/bg on scroll with IntersectionObserver
Don't: Jump from transparent to solid — feels broken
Code_Bad: window.onscroll = () => { if(scrollY > 100) header.style.background = 'white' }
A11y_Note: Ensure contrast meets WCAG in both states
Performance_Note: Use IntersectionObserver not scroll events for performance

Description: Large dropdown showing categorized links with previews
Do: Organize into columns by category with icons and descriptions
Don't: Open mega menu on hover only — inaccessible on mobile/touch
Code_Bad: <div onMouseOver={open}> — no keyboard or touch support
A11y_Note: All menu items keyboard-navigable with arrow keys
Performance_Note: Lazy load mega menu content on first interaction

Description: Keyboard-driven search overlay (Cmd+K) for power users
Do: Open with Cmd+K shortcut — search pages + actions + settings
Don't: Make it the only navigation method — casual users need visual nav
Code_Bad: No keyboard shortcut — only accessible via click
A11y_Note: aria-label='Command palette' role='dialog' aria-modal='true'
Performance_Note: Debounce search input — filter client-side for instant results

Description: Hierarchical path showing user's location in site structure
Do: Show breadcrumbs below header on interior pages
Don't: Use breadcrumbs on flat sites with no hierarchy
Code_Bad: Breadcrumbs with no links — defeats the purpose
A11y_Note: Use aria-current='page' on last item + structured data
Performance_Note: Minimal performance cost — render server-side for SEO

Description: Sidebar that expands/collapses between full and icon-only mode
Do: Persist collapsed state in localStorage — respect user preference
Don't: Auto-collapse without user action — disorienting
Code_Bad: Sidebar always full width on desktop — wastes space
A11y_Note: Icon-only mode needs tooltips with aria-label on each item
Performance_Note: Sidebar animation should be <200ms with ease-out curve

Description: Nested expandable tree for deep hierarchies (docs/file managers)
Do: Show expand/collapse chevrons — highlight active path
Don't: Expand all nodes by default — overwhelming
Code_Bad: <ul><li class='nested'> — no ARIA tree semantics
A11y_Note: role='tree' + role='treeitem' + aria-expanded required
Performance_Note: Virtualize trees with 100+ nodes for smooth performance

Description: Sidebar stays fixed while main content scrolls independently
Do: Use CSS grid with sidebar fixed and content overflow-y:auto
Don't: Use position:fixed on sidebar — breaks normal flow
Code_Bad: position:fixed on sidebar + margin-left on content — fragile layout
A11y_Note: Sidebar and content must be independently scrollable via keyboard
Performance_Note: Sticky positioning is more performant than fixed + margin

Description: iOS/Android style bottom navigation with 3-5 primary destinations
Do: Limit to 5 tabs maximum — more causes tiny tap targets
Don't: Put more than 5 items — use 'More' tab for overflow
Code_Bad: <nav> with 8 tiny bottom tabs — impossible to tap accurately
A11y_Note: Active tab: aria-current='page'. Tab icons need labels.
Performance_Note: Fixed bottom bar triggers layout shift — reserve space with pb-safe

Description: Three-line icon revealing off-canvas navigation drawer
Do: Animate hamburger → X on open. Use <button> not <div>
Don't: Use hamburger as only navigation on desktop — hide primary links
Code_Bad: <div class='hamburger' onclick='toggle()'> — not a button; no ARIA
A11y_Note: aria-expanded on trigger. Focus trap when drawer open.
Performance_Note: Drawer should slide in with transform not left/right animation

Description: Swipe down gesture to refresh content (mobile pattern)
Do: Show spinner + subtle haptic feedback during refresh
Don't: Trigger on tiny swipe — users refresh accidentally
Code_Bad: Refresh on any scroll up — broken UX
A11y_Note: Not accessible via keyboard — provide manual refresh button too
Performance_Note: Debounce to prevent double refresh. Cancel if swipe < threshold.

Description: Horizontal swipe to navigate between tabs/pages
Do: Add visual indicators (dots/tabs) showing swipeable pages
Don't: Allow swipe only — some users can't perform gesture
Code_Bad: <div onSwipe={changePage}> with no visual hint pages exist
A11y_Note: Provide prev/next buttons as keyboard-accessible alternative
Performance_Note: Use CSS scroll-snap for native performance over JS solutions

Description: Numbered page links with prev/next at ends
Do: Show current page + 2 neighbors + first/last with ellipsis
Don't: Show all 50 page numbers — overwhelming
Code_Bad: <div class='pages'> with 50 links
A11y_Note: aria-label='Pagination' on nav. aria-current='page' on active.
Performance_Note: Server-side pagination = fast. Client-side = requires all data loaded.

Description: Auto-load more content when scrolling near bottom
Do: Show loading skeleton at bottom + 'Load more' fallback button
Don't: Remove all pagination controls — user can't jump to specific content
Code_Bad: onscroll: if(scrollY + innerHeight >= scrollHeight) loadMore() — fires too often
A11y_Note: Announce new content via aria-live='polite' region
Performance_Note: Use IntersectionObserver not scroll event. Load 20-50 items per batch.

Description: Manual button to load next batch of content
Do: Show count remaining: 'Load 20 more (80 remaining)'
Don't: Hide the button when no more content — show 'All loaded'
Code_Bad: <button>Load more</button> with no count — user doesn't know how much is left
A11y_Note: Button is naturally keyboard accessible
Performance_Note: Lighter than infinite scroll — user controls when to load

Description: Search-as-you-type with live results dropdown
Do: Debounce input 200-300ms — show results in dropdown below search
Don't: Search on every keystroke — hammers server
Code_Bad: input.oninput = () => fetch(`/search?q=${input.value}`) — fires every character
A11y_Note: Use combobox pattern: role='combobox' + role='listbox'
Performance_Note: Debounce + abort previous request with AbortController

Description: Dedicated page showing filtered/sorted search results
Do: Show result count + active filters + sort options + clear all
Don't: Show results with no indication of total count or active filters
Code_Bad:
Results:
with no count or filters — feels incomplete
A11y_Note: Announce result count on search via aria-live region
Performance_Note: Paginate results server-side. Highlight matched terms client-side.

Description: Autocomplete dropdown showing popular/recent searches
Do: Show max 5-7 suggestions categorized (Recent / Popular / Products)
Don't: Show 20+ suggestions — choice paralysis
Code_Bad: <div class='suggestions'> with no ARIA roles
A11y_Note: role='listbox' on container + role='option' on items + aria-activedescendant
Performance_Note: Cache recent searches in localStorage for instant display

Description: Tab bar switching between content panels without page reload
Do: Use role='tablist' + role='tab' + role='tabpanel' + arrow key nav
Don't: Create tabs with links that reload page — breaks tab mental model
Code_Bad:
Tab 1
— link-based tabs reload
A11y_Note: Arrow keys navigate between tabs. Tab key moves to panel.
Performance_Note: Only render active tab panel — lazy mount inactive tabs

Description: Horizontally scrollable tab bar when too many tabs to fit
Do: Show scroll fade/arrow indicators at edges
Don't: Wrap tabs to second row — wastes space and looks cluttered
Code_Bad: <div class='flex flex-wrap'> — multi-row tabs confuse
A11y_Note: Ensure all tabs reachable via keyboard arrow keys
Performance_Note: Use scroll-snap for clean tab alignment on swipe

Description: Mobile drawer sliding up from bottom for actions/details
Do: Allow swipe-down to dismiss. Show drag handle indicator.
Don't: Cover entire screen — feels like page navigation not a sheet
Code_Bad: <div class='fixed inset-0'> — full screen takeover
A11y_Note: Focus trap when open. Escape key dismisses. aria-modal='true'.
Performance_Note: Transform animation only — avoid animating height.

Description: Side panel for details/settings without leaving current context
Do: Overlay panel with backdrop click-to-close
Don't: Navigate to new page for simple settings — breaks flow
Code_Good: <aside class='fixed right-0 top-0 h-full w-96 shadow-2xl transform transition'>
Code_Bad: <div style='position:absolute;right:-400px'> — left/right animation janky
A11y_Note: Focus trap inside panel. Return focus to trigger on close.
Performance_Note: Use transform:translateX for GPU-accelerated slide animation

Description: Highlight nav item matching current scroll position
Do: Use IntersectionObserver to detect active section
Don't: Calculate active section on every scroll event — janky
Code_Good: sections.forEach(s => observer.observe(s)); // update nav on intersect
Code_Bad: window.onscroll: sections.forEach(s => { if(s.offsetTop < scrollY)... }) — expensive
A11y_Note: Update aria-current on active nav link as user scrolls
Performance_Note: IntersectionObserver is far more performant than scroll + getBoundingClientRect

Description: Click anchor link to smoothly scroll to page section
Do: Use CSS scroll-behavior:smooth + scroll-margin-top for sticky header offset
Don't: Jump instantly to section — disorienting
Code_Bad: element.scrollIntoView() without smooth — jarring jump
A11y_Note: Respect prefers-reduced-motion: use instant scroll if reduced
Performance_Note: CSS smooth scroll is zero-JS and works natively

Description: Temporary non-blocking message appearing at screen edge
Do: Auto-dismiss after 5s with progress bar. Allow manual dismiss X.
Don't: Stack 10 toasts at once — covers content
Code_Bad: <div class='toast'> with no ARIA — screen readers miss it
A11y_Note: Use role='status' + aria-live='polite' for non-urgent toasts
Performance_Note: Limit to 3 visible toasts max. Queue the rest.

Description: Full-width persistent banner at top of page for important messages
Do: Use for critical system-wide alerts only. Include dismiss button.
Don't: Use banners for every notification — banner blindness
Code_Bad: <div class='banner'> for minor info — users start ignoring all banners
A11y_Note: role='alert' for urgent. role='status' for informational.
Performance_Note: Persistent banners shift layout — use fixed position to avoid CLS.

Description: Number badge on icon showing unread/pending count
Do: Show exact count up to 99 then '99+'. Position top-right of icon.
Don't: Show badge with 0 count — unnecessary visual noise
Code_Good: 3
Code_Bad: 0 — badge with zero is confusing
A11y_Note: aria-label on parent: 'Notifications (3 unread)'
Performance_Note: Badges are purely visual — negligible performance cost

Description: Progress bar showing current step in multi-step flow
Do: Show step numbers + labels + checkmark for completed steps
Don't: Show only current step with no context of total — user anxiety
Code_Bad:
Step 2
— no total count or step names
A11y_Note: Use aria-current='step' on active. Announce step change.
Performance_Note: Minimal cost. Render all step labels upfront for clarity.

Description: Break long form into digestible steps with save progress
Do: Save progress per step. Allow back navigation. Validate per step.
Don't: Validate all steps only on final submit — user finds errors in step 1 at step 5
Code_Bad: onSubmit: validateAll() — errors reference fields user can't see
A11y_Note: Announce step transitions. Focus first field of new step.
Performance_Note: Save draft to localStorage or server per step to prevent data loss.

Description: Hidden link to skip navigation and jump to main content
Do: First focusable element. Visible on focus. Links to #main-content.
Don't: Hide skip link permanently — keyboard users can't skip nav
Code_Good: Skip to content
Code_Bad: No skip link — keyboard user tabs through 50 nav items to reach content
A11y_Note: WCAG 2.4.1 requires skip mechanism. Must be first focusable element.
Performance_Note: Zero performance cost. Essential accessibility feature.

Description: Move focus to main content heading on SPA navigation
Do: Set focus to <h1> of new page after route change in SPA
Don't: SPA route changes with no focus management — screen reader stays on old content
Code_Bad: // no focus management on route change — SR user lost
A11y_Note: Announce page title via document.title + focus on h1
Performance_Note: Minimal cost. Critical for screen reader SPA navigation.

Description: Semantic HTML5 landmarks for page regions
Do: Use header/nav/main/aside/footer landmarks consistently
Don't: Use <div> for everything — no semantic structure
Code_Bad:
...
— no landmarks
A11y_Note: Screen readers can jump between landmarks (R key in NVDA)
Performance_Note: Zero cost. Landmarks are just semantic HTML elements.
